rules.description: Produce the most natural-sounding translation, capturing intended meaning and transferring it into natural language suited to a wide Romanian-speaking audience
rules.notes: Avoid literal transposition of English syntax in Romanian; sentences need to be rewritten with adapted syntax

rules.description: Use a clear and precise style that is easily understood and does not read like a translation
rules.notes: Balance faithfulness to source and readability in target language

rules.description: Avoid heaviness, ambiguity, repetitions and Anglicism. Also avoid enriching the text excessively
Translate buttons as verbs, generally using the imperative form. Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions
rules.notes: Pay attention to possible character/space limitations

rules.description: Use imperative form in button text for direct actions
rules.example_correct: Accesați pagina profilului și dați clic pe butonul Adăugați categorii
rules.example_incorrect: Accesați pagina profilului și dați clic pe butonul Adăugare categorii

rules.description: Simplify button text to essential action
rules.example_correct: Apăsați pe butonul OK pentru a salva toate modificările
rules.example_incorrect: Nu uitați să apăsați pe butonul În regulă pentru a salva toate modificările

rules.description: Use concise button text avoiding unnecessary explanatory text
rules.example_incorrect: Dacă doriți să revizuiți documentul, va trebui să îl descărcați

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions including terminology lists before translating product/application names, as many are trademarked
Replace English examples with Romanian equivalents when needed for localization/adaptation
rules.notes: Example given is about wildcard operators and their usage examples

rules.description: Use 2nd person plural (Formal) (dvs.)
Sound friendly and approachable without using terms that are TOO colloquial
rules.example_correct: Folosiți funcția de chat pentru a discuta cu prietenii
rules.example_incorrect: Folosiți funcția de chat pentru a sta la taclale cu prietenii

rules.description: Be precise and clear, consistent and natural. Rephrase unclear sentences and rework sentences that sound like a translation
rules.notes: Break up long English sentences into smaller friendlier parts for better understanding

rules.description: Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations
Use clear and coherent style so target reader understands the text easily
Use objective style: neutral, do not insert your own opinion, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct
Ensure content and numbers are correct and free of mistakes
Use idiomatically correct language with correct verbs, metaphors, expressions so text does not sound like a translation
Be complete: do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)
Keep an eye on the target reader: use an appropriate tone
Go through English text and interpret it before translating. Ensure understanding of overall meaning
rules.notes: Query any concepts that may be difficult to translate

rules.description: Beware of differences of register and tone between Romanian and American English. Use natural language
Avoid literal translations. Target language must flow naturally as if written originally in that language
Identify areas where language specific standard texts should be used (system requirements, legal text, etc.)
Translate internal cross-references consistently throughout translation and all files
rules.notes: References to chapters, sections, headings or titles within the file or document being translated

rules.description: Check if external documents have been localized before and match relevant translations unless otherwise specified
rules.notes: References to titles of other documents not being translated or already translated in previous project

rules.description: If document has not been translated before, leave external cross-reference in source language
rules.notes: Contact Project Management team or Language Lead if unable to retrieve translation information

rules.description: Use formal and polite style appropriate for business communication
Romanian usually gives priority to masculine forms, unless differently needed based on specific context
Try to rephrase translation to avoid needing to specify both gender forms whenever possible
rules.example_correct: Să fim prieteni! Spuneți-le tuturor despre noul nostru produs
rules.example_incorrect: Fiți prietenul(a) nostru(astră) și spuneți-le tuturor despre noul nostru produs

rules.description: Avoid gendered adjectives whenever possible
rules.example_correct: Ca utilizator, aveți acces la următoarele funcții
rules.example_incorrect: Ca utilizator(are), aveți acces la următoarele funcții
rules.notes: In English, sometimes 'they' is used as a generic third-person singular pronoun

rules.description: Avoid passive voice whenever possible and use active form to improve readability
rules.example_correct: În acest subiect, puteți găsi toate detaliile
rules.example_incorrect: Toate detaliile sunt furnizate în acest subiect
rules.notes: Passive voice is too formal and wordy

rules.description: Use passive voice only in specific texts like legal and official documents (privacy policies, terms of contract, NDAs, etc.)
Use active voice to make text more readable
rules.example_correct: Această carte albă detaliază anumite funcții noi
rules.example_incorrect: Anumite funcții noi sunt detaliate în această carte albă

rules.description: Do not transfer extensive use of English possessive adjectives to target language
rules.notes: In some cases, 'our' refers to a subject in the third person

rules.description: Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives
rules.example_correct: Puteți să vă editați fișierele fie pe computer, fie pe dispozitivul mobil
rules.example_incorrect: Dvs. puteți să editați fișierele dvs. fie pe computerul dvs., fie pe dispozitivul dvs. mobil

rules.description: For proper use of articles, refer to standard Romanian grammar rules
The –ing form can be translated in different ways based on context, such as verb, noun, infinitive clause
Use of gerund is not always recommendable in Romanian. Gerund is not correct when describing action occurring after or as consequence of another one or when acting like an adjective
rules.example_correct: Controlerele noastre au un cache care vă protejează integritatea datelor
rules.example_incorrect: Controlerele noastre vin cu un cache protejându-vă integritatea datelor

rules.description: Gerunds used in English headings and titles should never be translated as gerunds, but rather use noun form or verbal forms consistently
Use conditional structures instead of gerunds for sequential or conditional actions
rules.example_correct: Dacă accesați acest site web, sunteți de acord cu termenii și condițiile
rules.example_incorrect: Accesând acest site web, sunteți de acord cu termenii și condițiile

rules.description: Use appropriate Romanian introductory phrases instead of gerund forms
Check context, concentrate on meaning and render as clearly as possible. English sometimes uses additional words to express concepts
rules.example_correct: De acum, puteți accesa întregul conținut
rules.example_incorrect: Aceasta înseamnă că, de acum înainte, veți putea accesa întregul conținut

rules.description: Repetitions are acceptable in English but might not be in Romanian. Use pronouns or other techniques to avoid them
rules.example_correct: Pentru a șterge un fișier de pe computer, selectați-l din panoul din dreapta și dați clic pe Ștergeți
rules.example_incorrect: Pentru a șterge un fișier de pe computer, selectați fișierul din panoul din dreapta și dați clic pe Ștergeți

rules.description: Be careful with false friends and literal translations that can have different meaning from source
rules.notes: For English 'Abort'

rules.description: Translate 'Actually' correctly
Translate 'Assume' correctly
Translate 'Billion' correctly
Translate 'Consistent' correctly
Translate 'Fabric' correctly
Translate 'Library' correctly
Translate 'Resume' correctly
Translate 'Support' correctly
Refer to Romanian extensive capitalization rules in DOOM 3
In Romanian titles, only the first letter of the first word is capitalized
When title contains a proper noun, brand name, feature, app etc., that term is also capitalized
When title contains the name of a page, that name is capitalized
rules.notes: Here, Prezentare generală is the name of a page

rules.description: Apply general capitalization rule to UIs and table headers
Romanian does NOT capitalize the name of days and months
Do not capitalize days of the week
Use capital letter for proper names, product names, geographical names
Use capital letter for the first word after a period, exclamation mark, question mark, after ellipsis when the sentence starting after ellipsis is different or repetition of previous sentence
Use capital letter after colon only when sentence is a quote, explanatory sentence (in enumerations) or when sentence is a note, notice, advertisement or warning
In title case, use capitals for nouns and adjectives of organizations, bodies, departments, awards, and events
rules.notes: Examples: United Nations, Internal Affairs

rules.description: Use lower case for nationalities, languages, religions, days of the week, months, season names, currencies, job titles in Romanian
Use lower case for months
rules.example_correct: Va fi gata până în iulie
rules.example_incorrect: Va fi gata până în Iulie

rules.description: Use lower case for currencies
Use lower case for languages
Use lower case for nationalities
In titles, headings, UI options, only the first word of the string should be capitalized
Capitalize only first word in terms and conditions titles
Capitalize only first word in UI elements
Capitalize only first word in model names
Exception to capitalization rule: in legal texts, capitalization conveys specific meaning and must be retained in translation
rules.example_correct: Acest contract („Contractul") este un acord cu efecte juridice
rules.example_incorrect: Acest contract („contractul") este un acord cu efecte juridice

rules.description: Preserve capitalization in legal software terms
rules.example_correct: Instalați și folosiți o copie a Software-ului
rules.example_incorrect: Instalați și folosiți o copie a software-ului

rules.description: Preserve formatting when whole paragraphs are written in all upper case as these conventions may have legal implications
Use ASCII code Alt+0147 for opening English quotation mark
Use ASCII code Alt+0148 for closing English quotation mark
Use ASCII code Alt+0171 for opening French quotation mark
Use ASCII code Alt+0187 for closing French quotation mark
Run spell-checker when translation is completed
Use new (standard) Romanian extended characters, specifically ș Ș, ț Ț
Use comma to separate decimals
Use full-stop to separate thousands
Ensure consistent use of spaces in target documents, even if source document is inconsistent
rules.notes: Remove double spaces and be particularly aware of non-breaking spaces around tags

rules.description: Measurement units are separated from the value by a space (preferably non-breaking space)
The percentage symbol % is usually not separated by a space
Brand name tags need to be separated from preceding or following text with a space
In Romanian there is no space before any punctuation sign
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in Romanian
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent certain expressions from being separated at end of line (numbers + measurement units, software names and versions, etc.)
rules.notes: Press Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or use Alt+0160

rules.description: Use comma when a natural pause is heard, but use of comma is quite subjective
In enumeration, each element should be separated by comma. The last element is preceded by conjunction and no comma
In a sentence, the subject should not be separated by comma from the predicate
A vocative particle must be always preceded and/or followed by a comma
Use period to end a sentence, paragraph or abbreviated word
When sentence is placed between brackets or quotes, the period is placed outside
Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in a table, or after captions used with illustrations, tables etc., unless the expression is a full sentence
Do not use full stops if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation or exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Try to avoid using colons, as English makes wider use of them. Prefer commas wherever possible
Colon is commonly used to introduce new sentences (enumerations, explanations, notices, advertisements, warnings)
Use uppercase after headings like 'Avertisment', 'Atenție', 'Pericol', 'Notă', etc. when the statement is a full sentence
Semi-colons are used to separate sentences or groups of sentences that are syntactically independent but semantically related
Avoid use of semi-colons. Start a new sentence instead
Exclamation marks are not as common in Romanian as in English. Avoid using exclamation marks unless really necessary
rules.notes: As per voice and style

rules.description: When exclamation marks appear in groups (!!!), keep only one

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Hyphen (-) is used to divide words between syllables, link parts of compound word, connect parts of inverted or imperative verb form, articulate loanwords with ends differing from pronunciation
rules.notes: It is not preceded or followed by space

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: En dash (–) is used to structure sentence, has role of parentheses and isolates elements not essential to sentence meaning
rules.example_correct: Apoziția reprezintă o explicație, izolată în enunț prin semne de punctuație, care are rolul de a suplimenta – din punct de vedere semantic – un mesaj
rules.notes: It is preceded and followed by space

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Em dash (—) is only used in specific cases (linie de dialog)

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Round and square brackets
rules.description: In Romanian round brackets are commonly seen, and square brackets are only used when round brackets already appear in the sentence
rules.notes: Bear in mind that often square brackets are used to define tags

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
Romanian uses Alt+0132 for opening quotation mark („) and Alt+0148 for closing quotation mark (")
rules.notes: In software strings, Romanian quotation marks do not need to be escaped

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: Lists can be introduced by lead-in phrases or clauses, introduced by constructions ended with a colon

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: In both bulleted and numbered lists, list items end with semicolon (except for very short items with no verb) and are not capitalized (except for proper nouns)

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: Exception: If items in list are very short with no verb, such as single words or phrases with no internal punctuation, put comma at end of each item
rules.example_correct: act de identitate, cerere, dosar cu șină.
rules.notes: Rule of thumb: these lists include simple items that could dwell in a sentence as enumeration

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: For longer list items with no verb, follow standard punctuation for lists

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: Standard list rules apply for lists introduced by sentence ending in colon
rules.example_correct: Pentru a vă actualiza numărul de mobil, așteptăm să ne trimiteți: numărul preferat, cu prefixul țării; numele complet, așa cum apare în actul de identitate; data expirării cardului de credit adăugat în profil.

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If list item contains more than one sentence, first sentence starts with small letter and last sentence ends with full stop. All other sentences follow regular editing rules
rules.example_correct: numele trebuie să fie vizibil pe document; imaginile încărcate trebuie să fie clare și lizibile. Nu folosiți blițul când fotografiați documentele. Blițul creează o pată de lumină care poate acoperi informații importante. verificați dacă toate cele patru colțuri ale documentelor sunt vizibile în fotografiile încărcate.

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: For lists not introduced by constructions ended with colon, which usually include standalone items, use regular text editing standards and punctuation
rules.example_correct: Pentru a începe, selectați Profiluri. Selectați „Plată
rules.notes: Even when semantically introduced by a sentence in text, if that sentence does not end with colon, list items start with capital letter and end with full stop

rules.description: Meta descriptions have maximum of 155 characters (including spaces and punctuation). Should not be shorter than 50 characters or longer than 155 characters
rules.notes: As long as description accurately describes content of page and follows rule of thumb, should be okay

rules.description: Meta titles have maximum of 55 characters (including spaces and punctuation)
Follow title case capitalization/formatting rules for meta titles
Separate 'RingCentral' from rest of meta title with horizontal bar
Use en dash (–) to separate keywords/keyword phrases
Abbreviations should be avoided whenever possible, but may be needed in UI options. Preferably use standardized abbreviations
Use SUA for United States of America
Use Etc. for Etcetera
Use UK for United Kingdom
Use Max. for Maximum
Use Min. for Minimum
Use Pt. for Pentru
Use Tel. for Telefon
Use Nr. for Număr
Use Pag. for Pagină
Use De ex. for De exemplu
Use UE for Uniunea Europeană
Translation of acronyms depends on RingCentral project-related requirements and should always be investigated to confirm localizability
Acronyms should not have plural endings
When acronym comes before its expansion, keep this order
When expansion comes before its acronym, keep this order
Write numbers in full when used as nouns
Write numbers in full when at the beginning of a sentence
Write numbers in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually the numbers up to 9
Write numbers in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'
Write dates, large numbers, ages, document references (paragraph, page, etc.) as numbers
Replace decimal point with comma and thousand separator with point
Use proper Romanian number formatting for large numbers
Use proper formatting for thousands
Use proper formatting for millions
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Units of measure should be separated from the preceding number by a non-breaking space
Convert imperial units to metric based on project requirements, either adding converted value first with original in brackets or converting to metric equivalent
rules.notes: Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches and represent an exception

rules.description: Use conversion: 1 inch = 2,54 cm
Use conversion: 1 foot = 30,48 cm
Use conversion: 1 yard = 91,44 cm
Use conversion: 1 mile = 1,609 km
Use conversion: 1 pint = 0,57 l
Use conversion: 1 quart = 1,136 l
Use conversion: 1 gallon = 4,546 l
Use conversion: 1 ounce = 28,349 g
Use conversion: 1 pound = 453,59 g
Use conversion: 1 stone = 6,348 kg
Use conversion: 1 ton = 1.016 kg
Percentages are usually written in figures followed by the symbol %. No blank spaces should be used between the figure and the symbol
The correct date order for Romanian is DD.MM.YYYY (day first)
If a month needs to be abbreviated, use the first three letters and end in a period, except for September (Sept.)
November is abbreviated as nov., to create no confusion with 'noi' (which is a pronoun)
Use ian. for ianuarie
Use feb. for februarie
Use mar. for martie
Use apr. for aprilie
Use mai for mai (no abbreviation)
Use iun. for iunie
Use iul. for iulie
Use aug. for august
Use sept. for septembrie
Use oct. for octombrie
Use nov. for noiembrie
Use dec. for decembrie
The correct time format for Romanian is: 04:00, 16:00 (use 24-hour format, without am/pm)
Currency symbols are placed after the numeral and preceded by a non-breaking space, with two decimal figures
Romanian uses ISO codes for currency
Use 100 CAD for Canadian dollar
Use 100 EUR for European currency, not €100 EUR
Use 100 GBP for Great Britain pound, not £100 GBP
Use 100 USD for US dollar
Translate 'About' as 'Despre'
Translate 'as shown in figure xx' as 'după cum se arată în figura xx'
rules.example_correct: după cum se arată în figura xx

rules.description: Translate 'For more information on xx, see xxx.' as 'Pentru mai multe informații despre xx, consultați xxx.'
rules.example_correct: Pentru mai multe informații despre xx, consultați xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'N/A (Non Available)' as 'Indisponibil'
Translate 'Please note that' as 'Rețineți'
Translate 'Refer to xx for instructions on xxx.' as 'Consultați xx pentru instrucțiuni despre xx'
rules.example_correct: Consultați xx pentru instrucțiuni despre xx

rules.description: Translate 'registered trademark' as 'marcă comercială înregistrată'
Translate 'Troubleshooting' as 'Depanare'
Translate 'Trademark' as 'Marcă comercială'
Translate 'User's Guide' as 'Ghid de utilizare'
Translate 'want (to), would like' as 'dori'
Translate 'we recommend…' as 'vă recomandăm'
Translate 'Follow these steps to xxx' as 'Urmați acești pași pentru a xxx'
rules.example_correct: Urmați acești pași pentru a xxx

rules.description: Refer to the most updated version of the approved RingCentral term list for your language
Use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on the first mention in the translated version of your content, unless otherwise indicated

For third party products mentioned in RingCentral content, complete extensive research to determine whether an official, approved trademarked translated version is available; otherwise, use as in source
Copyright notices tend to be standard texts. Make sure you receive instructions on whether an official and approved translated version is available
rules.notes: Example provided: ©2016 RingCentral, Inc. All rights reserved. RingCentral, RingCentral Office, RingCentral Meetings, and the RingCentral logo are registered trademarks of RingCentral, Inc. Other third-party marks and logos displayed in this document are the trademarks of their respective owners.

rules.description: Use the copyright symbol to denote copyright ownership for works other than sound recordings
rules.notes: To create a copyright symbol on a Mac, press opt + [ g ]. In Windows, hold alt and type 0169. When a copyright symbol is needed in plain-text format, use a lowercase 'c' in parentheses: (c) 2016 RingCentral

rules.description: When source files include references to URLs, translators should query if said link has a local version, and modify the document accordingly
rules.notes: If not, the relevant URL will be left as it appears in the source English text

rules.description: If software has been translated, match the relevant translation memory or string list provided with the translation package
If software has not been translated, use bilingual format, i.e. English first, followed by target in brackets
Use bold type to reference on-screen instructions, commands, menu items, or buttons
When necessary, refer to Microsoft approved terminology for your language on the MS Portal
rules.notes: http://www.microsoft.com/Language/en-US/Default.aspx

rules.description: Translate country and city names based on your country/region's conventions
Localize address formats, if necessary, based on your country/region's conventions
Unless needed for clarity, don't capitalize web or email addresses
Do not include www or http/s in URLs
If a URL comes at the end of the sentence, use a period after. Be sure the period is not part of a hyperlinked URL
If source only includes toll free numbers and US numbers, add an international country code to the local number for international users
rules.example_correct: RingCentral, Inc. (Belmont/HQ) 20 Davis Drive, Belmont, California 94402 Teléfono: +1-888-528-RING (7464) o +1-650-472-4100
rules.example_incorrect: RingCentral, Inc. (Belmont/HQ) 20 Davis Drive, Belmont, California 94402 Phone: 888-528-RING (7464) or 650-472-4100

rules.description: If a term is not listed in this section, check the common and industry-specific terms section
rules.notes: Unless otherwise indicated, use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on the first mention

rules.description: Romanian uses metric system, not imperial
Units of measurement are separated from the value by a space (preferably non-breaking)
The percentage isn't separated by a space
